explanations: Some masculine nouns have two bases ending in -u and -ar. They express the agent or a relationship. An example is satthu / satthar meaning 'teacher' (lit., he who admonishes).
Though bhattu (husband) and nattu (grandson) are nouns expressing relationships, they are declined like agent nouns such as satthā, as in Sanskrit.
Masculine nouns expressing relationships such as pitu (father) and bhātu (brother) are declined somewhat differently from agent nouns.
Dhītu (daughter) and duhitu (daughter) are declined similarly to pitu and bhātu.
Feminine nouns expressing relationships such as mātu / mātar (mother) have their own declension pattern with additional alternative forms in several cases.

tables.title: Declension of satthu / satthar (teacher) - masculine agent noun
Declension of pitu / pitar (father) - masculine relationship noun
Declension of bhātu / bhātar (brother) - masculine relationship noun
Declension of mātu / mātar (mother) - feminine relationship noun

pali_to_english: Satthā bhikkhūnaṃ dhammaṃ desento rukkhassa chāyāya nisinno hoti.
Puññāni kattāro bhikkhūnaṃ ca tāpasānaṃ ca dānaṃ denti.
Sace satthā dhammaṃ deseyya viññātāro bhavissanti.
Bhūpati dīpasmiṃ jetā bhavatu.
Pitā dhītaraṃ ādāya vihāraṃ gantvā satthāraṃ vandāpesi.
Viññātāro loke manussānaṃ netāro hontu / bhavantu.
Bhātā pitarā saddhiṃ mātuyā pacitaṃ yāguṃ bhuñji.
Bhattā nattārehi saha kīḷantaṃ kapiṃ disvā hasanto aṭṭhāsi (stood).
Setuṃ kattāro veḷavo bandhitvā nadiyā tīre ṭhapesuṃ.
Sindhuṃ taritvā dīpaṃ gantāro sattūhi hatā honti.
Bhariyā bhattu sāṭake rajakena dhovāpesi.
Netuno kathaṃ sotāro uyyāne nisinnā suriyena pīḷitā honti.
Dātārehi dinnāni vatthāni yācakehi na vikkiṇitabbāni honti.
Rodantassa nattussa kujjhitvā vanitā taṃ (him) hatthena pahari.
Vinetuno ovādaṃ (advice) sutvā bandhavo sappurisā abhaviṃsu / ahesuṃ.
Gehesu ca aṭavīsu ca vasante ākhavo ahayo khādanti.
Nattā mātaraṃ yāguṃ yācanto bhūmiyaṃ patitvā rodati.
Tumhe bhātarānaṃ ca bhaginīnaṃ ca mā kujjhatha.
Dīpaṃ gantārehi nāvāya sindhu taritabbo hoti.
Pubbakā (ancient) isayo mantānaṃ (magic spells) kattāro ca mantānaṃ pavattāro (reciters) ca abhaviṃsu / ahesuṃ.
Mattaññū dātā nattārānaṃ thokaṃ thokaṃ modake (sweets) dadiṃsu / adaṃsu.
Atthaññū netāro manusse sappurise karontā vinetāro bhavanti.
Mātā dhītaraṃ ovadantī sīsaṃ (head) cumbitvā (kissed) bāhuṃ āmasitvā samassāsesi.
Vadaññū brāhmaṇo khudāya pīḷente yācake disvā pahūtaṃ (much) bhojanaṃ (food) dāpesi.
Sārathinā āhaṭe veḷavo gahetvā vaḍḍhakī sālaṃ māpesi.
english_to_pali: Father and mother went with the brother to see the sister.
Evil doers will not live long happily.
May the king, together with his retinue, become victorious.
The enemies of my brothers tied banners on trees and bamboos.
The house builder gave bamboos to the grandsons.
Brother gave food to (my) daughter with a spoon.
The Buddha is the teacher of gods and men.
May you (pl.) be speakers of the truth.
Good husbands are kind (kāruṇikā) to their wives like gods.
Let good men become powerful ministers to govern the island.
